analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (counterparty IDs): forward mid moves after prints, stratified by aggressor inference (price vs BBO), K in {5,20,100} tape rows, spread quantile (tight/mid/wide on traded symbol), burst flag, hour; pair-cell baseline means; buyer→seller graph; burst vs random-time extract controls; consecutive-trade two-hop triple counts; adverse aggressor summary.
used_to_decide: Tape days 1–3 per Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4. Horizon K = K rows forward in price CSV for same (day, symbol). See round4_phase1_complete for gate checklist.

round4_phase1_complete.horizon_definition: K \in {5,20,100} means K rows forward in prices_round_4_day_*.csv after the row matching (day, timestamp, symbol); forward mid change = mid(t+K) - mid(t).

bullets.conclusion: Strongest signal: **Mark 67 aggressive buys** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** in **tight** spread_q show large positive mean forward mids (K=5: mean≈1.98, t≈12.7, n≈154; K=20 similar). **Mark 55 aggressive sells** extract at K=100 mid spread show **negative** mean fwd (≈-0.94, p≈0.015, n≈538). **Mark 22** sell_agg on **VEV_5300** tight+**burst** K=5: negative mean (≈-0.20, p≈0.001, n=160). Mark 01 buy_agg on VEV_5200 tight had n=0 in per-day slice (trades often inside spread → unk).

bullets.topic: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pair × symbol × K × regime cell means)
bullets.conclusion: Residuals vs (buyer|seller, symbol, K, spread_q, burst) cell means are **~machine zero** everywhere in the top table — dominant structure is **explained by the coarse pair/regime cells**; no large stable second-order residual in this parameterization.

Graph matches prior sketch: **Mark 01→Mark 22** dominates (1339 prints). Consecutive-trade two-hop buyer→seller→(next seller) shows **Mark 14→Mark 38→Mark 14** and **Mark 38→Mark 14→Mark 38** oscillation (≈165 / 160) — **duopoly ping-pong**, not a one-way insider chain.

Multi-print timestamps (n≈774 burst events): mean **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** forward mid change is **higher** after bursts than random same-day controls for K=20 (burst mean ≈0.33 vs control ≈0.11; K=5 also higher). Mid-only; tradable PnL not tested here.

**Mark 38 buy_agg** and **Mark 55 sell_agg** have the **most negative** mean 20-tick same-symbol markouts among marks with n≥15; **Mark 67 buy_agg** most positive — consistent with long-extract pressure after Mark 67 lifts.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: After **multi-symbol burst** at a timestamp, **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** mid tends to drift **up** more than random controls over **K=20** tape rows (effect ≈0.22 mid ticks; n_bursts=774 vs n_ctrl=2321).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Mid not bid/ask; confirm with --match-trades worse sim before sizing.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 67** **aggressive buy** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** when extract BBO is **tight** → large **positive** forward mids at K=5/20/100 (n≈154; t≥5).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Low-frequency actor vs Mark 55; watch overfit.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 55 aggressive sell** on extract in **mid** spread → **negative** mean forward at **K=100** (mean≈-0.94, p≈0.015, n≈538).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Long horizon K=100 overlaps signals; use for regime not precise timing.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 22** **aggressive sell** on **VEV_5300** in **tight+burst** → **negative** short (K=5) forward on same symbol (t≈-3.3, n=160).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Tick discretization; effect is sub-tick mean.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 55 aggressive buy** on extract in **mid** spread → **weak positive** K=20 mean (≈+0.32, p≈0.07, n≈546) — possible MM inventory mean-revert opposite to sell leg at K=100.
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Marginal p; not significant at K=5.

negative_findings: **Mark 01 buy_agg on VEV_5200 tight K=20** has **n=0** per day in this join (prints rarely at touch) — cannot validate Sonic-style 5200 pipe on aggressive-buy subset without inside-spread model.
**Pair-cell residuals** show **no** meaningful deviation from (pair, symbol, K, regime) means — second-order ‘mystery alpha’ not visible in this baseline.
**Two-hop chains** are dominated by **14↔38→14** alternation — explains **liquidity recycling**, not directional extract alpha by itself.

analysis_description: Phase 2: (1) Mark01→Mark22 multi-VEV burst Type-B conditioning vs controls + per-day OOS; (2) microprice gap vs forward vol proxy; (3) signed extract flow lead-lag vs extract d_mid; hydro flow vs extract; (5) VEV_5200 IV residual vs Mark01/22 VEV prints; (7) Mark55 cumulative aggressive flow vs extract mid.
used_to_decide: See round4_phase2_complete for Phase-1 interaction and ranked edges.

round4_phase2_complete.phase1_interaction: Type-B Mark01→Mark22 multi-VEV bursts **refine** generic multi-print burst story: extract fwd20 mean **+0.24** vs control **-0.13** pooled, but **day 1 is negative (-0.34)** while **day 3 is strongly positive (+0.96)** — Phase-1 generic burst edge is **not day-stable**; Mark67/Mark55 patterns from Phase 1 **orthogonal** (actor-level) and persist in Phase 2 IV residual (M01/M22 prints coincide with **lower** IV residual on 5200, Welch p≈10^-80). Microprice→vol edge is **null** (~0 corr).

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: At timestamps where **Mark 01→Mark 22** prints **≥2 different VEV symbols**, **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** forward mid (K=20 tape rows) **beats** random-time controls on **pooled** days 1–3 (mean **+0.24** vs **-0.13**; n=217 bursts).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Tightens Phase-1 generic burst to **named basket** choreography; **falsifies day-stability** (day1 negative, day3 hot).

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Mark 01/Mark 22 VEV prints** align with **lower** rolling IV residual on **VEV_5200** (mean residual **-0.0048** vs **+0.0002** otherwise; Welch t≈-25.8).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: **Confirms** structural basket pressure on smile level; not same as mid markout but **stackable** with pair conditioning.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Signed extract flow** shows **positive** correlation with **next-tick** extract mid change (lag1 corr **≈+0.115**), near-zero at lag0 negative.
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Orthogonal to counterparty tables; supports **short-horizon momentum** after aggressive flow aggregation.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Cumulative Mark 55** aggressive signed flow on extract correlates with extract mid within each tape day (mean per-day corr **≈+0.33** over 3 days).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Inventory-style interpretation consistent with Phase-1 Mark55 buy/sell asymmetry at long K.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Hydro signed flow** vs extract **Δmid** at **lead-1** tick: corr **≈+0.030** (weak cross-leg; same-tick ~0).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Adds cross-instrument dimension beyond Phase-1 single-asset markouts.

null_or_negative: **Microprice minus mid** vs forward 5-tick absolute mid volatility: corr **≈ -0.0014** (no microprice edge at L1). File: r4_phase2_microprice_spread_vol.json.
**Regime-only** microstructure signal not found without richer book than L1 tape.
round4_phase2_complete.sim_note: trader_v0.py implements one Phase-2 execution hook (skip extract ask when Mark38 aggressive buy seen in market_trades); backtest worse total **4664** on days 1–3 (not a profit goal).
